items.title: Spot Prices Wrap: Northeast Cold Unwinds, Western Heat Wave Arrives to Split Natural Gas Markets
items.summary: Spot natural gas prices were mixed Tuesday as an unwinding cold spell in the Northeast and Midwest contrasted with record challenging heat building in the West.

items.title: Futures Settle: Natural Gas Futures Hold $3 Line as Weather, Supply Tug-of-War Persists
items.summary: April natural gas futures edged higher Tuesday during a choppy session, with a late-season cold snap supporting demand even as warming trends and strong production signaled looser balances ahead during the shoulder season.

items.title: MidDay Snapshot: Regional Weather Swings Drive Mixed Spot Gas Prices Across Lower 48
items.summary: Spot natural gas prices for Wednesday delivery were mixed as unseasonably warm weather lifted Western markets, while shifting weather patterns softened prices across most Midwest and East hubs.

items.title: LNG Buildout Must Continue to Match Growing Demand Through 2050, Shell Says
items.summary: Even after record investments across the industry in recent years, more LNG export capacity will need to be built across the world in the 2030s and 2040s to keep pace with even the lowest demand forecasts, according to Shell plc’s latest market analy

items.title: South Texas Gas Hub Sees Pricing Boost Amid Iran War
items.summary: Natural gas prices at Agua Dulce in South Texas have strengthened while narrowing their discount to Henry Hub since conflict began in the Middle East.

items.title: Why Permian Basin Natural Gas Prices Can’t Catch a Break
items.summary: Benchmark Permian Basin natural gas prices have traded in negative territory far more than not in 2026 — and there are no signs of relief in the near term.

items.title: Mild Weather, Wind Crush Natural Gas Demand With Power Burns Near Yearly Low
items.summary: Natural gas demand weakened sharply during the latest storage week as springlike weather and surging wind generation cut into heating and power loads, setting up an early season injection in Thursday’s U.S. Energy Information Administration (EIA) rep

items.title: MidDay Futures: How Long Can $3 Floor Hold for Natural Gas Futures as Bearish Weather Signals Persist?
items.summary: April Nymex natural gas futures held modest gains in sluggish midday trading Tuesday, with support near $3.00/MMBtu intact, though warmer weather signals pointed to further downside risk.

items.title: Midmorning Markets: As U.S. Demand Fades, Western Canada Natural Gas Prices Weaken
items.summary: Natural gas spot prices in Western Canada shifted lower at the start of this week as U.S. demand for imports fell far from winter highs and was poised to decline further.

items.title: Natural Gas Pipeline Capacity Must Grow 39% as LNG, Data Center Demand Surge, INGAA Says
items.summary: North America needs nearly 40,000 miles of new natural gas pipeline and a 39% increase in transmission capacity to meet rising demand by 2052, part of a more than $1 trillion midstream buildout spanning natural gas, oil, natural gas liquids, hydrogen

items.title: U.S. Has Used Up Most Options to Soften the Oil Price Shock
items.summary: The Trump Administration has already used up most emergency options to rein in the soaring international crude oil prices, which have pushed up U.S. gasoline prices by $0.80 per gallon from a month ago. The U.S. last week tapped the Strategic Petrole

items.title: Standard Chartered Predicts Oil Prices Will Remain Higher For Longer
items.summary: On Monday, European Union foreign ministers rejected demands by U.S. President Donald Trump to help secure the Strait of Hormuz through military means, with Europe only keen on further bolstering the security of its own military bases in the region.

items.title: Skyrocketing Gas Prices Threaten Political Order Across the Globe

items.title: Why Taking Over Utilities Won’t Deliver Cheap Electricity
items.summary: Renewable energy sources not only disrupt business relationships due to their low prices and rapid deployment, but they might also disrupt political movements as well, specifically efforts on the part of local governments to take over investor-owned

items.title: Japanese Refiner Idemitsu Moves Full-Scale into LNG Business
items.summary: Japan’s petroleum refiner Idemitsu Kosan has committed $500 million to LNG investment company MidOcean Energy as part of MidOcean’s equity raise of $1.2 billion, as the Japanese refining giant is looking to enter the global LNG business on a full-sca
